justification: B outperforms A on every dimension. On skill_accuracy, A includes 'Computer Vision', 'Deep Learning', 'Machine Learning', and 'Data Preprocessing' as standalone skills — these are either course names or inferred abstractions not listed as discrete skills in the resume's SKILLS section, and 'Machine Learning' and 'Data Preprocessing' are not explicitly listed at all. B sticks to the explicitly listed skills and correctly excludes Jupyter (not demonstrated in projects) while keeping the rest well-evidenced. On conservatism, A bloats to 20 skills including vague abstractions; B lands at 16 well-grounded skills, closer to the ideal range. On project_classification, A extracts no projects at all (neutral score of 3), while B correctly classifies both projects as 'AI/ML (Computer Vision)' and appropriately does not label the React integration as a Full-Stack app. On impact_quality, A extracts no impact highlights whatsoever, missing the deployment URL, the 12% mAP improvement, and the 28 FPS metric — all of which are explicitly called out in the expected behavior. B captures all key metrics with specificity including the live deployment URL, baseline comparison, FPS, accuracy benchmarks, and dataset scale.
